name: Reliability V1
description: Improve pass rate on hard/timeout cases. Each hypothesis targets a specific failure cluster from WEBBENCH-50 diagnosis.

hypotheses.name: Graceful execute timeout recovery
hypotheses.rationale: 3/5 WEBBENCH-50 failures (AllTrails, AliExpress, SportingNews) abort after 3 consecutive execute wall-clock timeouts. The agent never gets a chance to adapt. Recovery: catch timeouts, re-observe, inject feedback, let agent try a different approach. Cap at 2 recoveries to prevent infinite loops.
hypotheses.risk: Could waste 2 extra turns on truly broken pages. Mitigated by 2-recovery cap.

treatment._note: Code change in src/runner/runner.ts — no config delta needed. The fix catches execute timeouts in the inner catch block and recovers instead of propagating to the crash handler.

hypotheses.name: Adaptive page-ready detection for heavy SPAs
hypotheses.rationale: AliExpress and AllTrails have infinite JS hydration loops. The 5s domcontentloaded cap helps but the Playwright action still waits for network idle. Adding a shorter action timeout for known-slow patterns (SPA frameworks, heavy CDN) could let the agent interact with partially loaded pages earlier.
hypotheses.expected_impact: pass rate: +1-2pp, duration: -10% on SPA-heavy sites
hypotheses.risk: Interacting with partially loaded pages may cause stale selectors. Mitigated by existing stale-ref recovery.

treatment._note: Code change: detect SPA patterns in observe, reduce Playwright action timeout for SPA pages

hypotheses.name: Temporal article filtering for news/sports sites
hypotheses.rationale: Goal.com failure (webbench-695): agent spent 19 turns and 495k tokens clicking preview articles instead of match analysis. Adding date-awareness to article selection would help distinguish pre-match from post-match content.
hypotheses.expected_impact: pass rate: +1pp (Goal.com), broader benefit on news sites
hypotheses.risk: Date parsing heuristics may be fragile across different site formats.

treatment._note: Code change: extract article dates from snapshot, prefer recent published articles over upcoming-event previews
